title: tetragonia pedunculata
intro: Tetragonia pedunculata, also known as kare Kare or New Zealand spinach, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand and parts of Australia. It is a succulent plant with edible leaves and stems, rich in vitamins and minerals, often used as a substitute for spinach. The leaves of Tetragonia pedunculata have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or juice, and its leaves can also be eaten raw in salads or cooked as a vegetable, providing several health benefits, including improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function.
